_meta.purpose: Default planning assumptions for the EXW -> shelf pricing walk. Every number here is a transparent, overridable default. Daley's trade data (distributor cost sheets, importer quotes, state price postings, live FX) should override these before a $5,000 strategy goes out.
_meta.disclaimer: Planning defaults only. Duty, excise, FX, and freight are volatile and route-specific. VERIFY each against a current source before sign-off. Not tax or legal advice.

conventions.fx: EXW is converted to the target-market currency using fx.rates (base = target currency). Update fx.rates and fx.asOf before every run.
conventions.caseSize: All per-case figures assume the case's bottleCount x bottleMl. Default case = 12 x 750ml = 9.0 litres.
conventions.marginVsMarkup: Trade-tier margins are expressed as marginPctOnSell (margin on the tier's OWN selling price). sell = cost / (1 - marginPctOnSell). This matches how importers and distributors quote.
conventions.onPremise: onPremiseMultiplier multiplies the FRONT-LINE (wholesale-to-trade) bottle price to give the restaurant by-the-bottle list price.
conventions.offPremise: offPremiseMarginPctOnSell is the retailer's margin on their shelf price: shelf = frontline / (1 - margin).
conventions.tax: Consumer sales tax / VAT is shown separately at the shelf. Trade tiers reclaim VAT, so it is NOT compounded through the chain except where a market is VAT-inclusive at trade (noted per market).

fx.note: Indicative only — replace with live mid-market rates before use. Base is the amount of target currency per 1 unit of the listed currency.

US.label: United States
US.freightNote: Ocean LCL, EU -> US East Coast, per 9L case. Highly variable; get a current quote.

duty.note: Sparkling and >14% differ. Confirm current HTS line.

excise.federalBasis: US federal excise $1.07/gallon (<=16% ABV) = $0.2827/litre. CBMA small-producer credits can reduce this.
excise.stateNote: State excise varies widely and may be levied at distributor or retail tier. Examples are indicative.

US.controlStatesNote: PA, UT, NC, VA, AL, ID, MS, MT, NH, OH, OR, VT, WV, WY and others run control systems where the state is wholesaler and/or retailer; the importer/distributor split above does not apply cleanly.

UK.label: United Kingdom
UK.freightNote: EU -> UK groupage per 9L case post-Brexit; includes customs handling. Get a current quote.

duty.basis: Still wine 11.5-14.5% ABV under the 2023 alcohol duty system (transitional easement).
duty.note: From the Feb 2025 move to strict by-ABV bands this varies by exact ABV (~£2.45-£2.88 / 75cl). Confirm against HMRC for the wine's ABV.

excise.note: UK 'duty' above already includes excise; no separate line.

UK.distributorNote: Many UK routes collapse importer+wholesaler into one margin; set distributor margin to 0 if a single intermediary.

consumerTax.note: 20% VAT on the duty-inclusive shelf price; trade reclaims input VAT.

FR.label: France
FR.freightNote: Intra-EU road freight per 9L case (nil if origin is France). Get a current quote.

duty.basis: Intra-EU movements of EU-origin wine attract no customs duty.
duty.note: Non-EU origin wine entering France attracts EU import duty + may need an importer of record.

excise.basis: French 'droit de circulation' on still wine ~ EUR 3.92/hL = ~EUR 0.0294 / 75cl.
excise.note: Among the lowest in the EU.

FR.distributorNote: Caviste (specialist off-trade) margins often run 30-35%; set offPremise accordingly for that channel.

CA.label: Canada
CA.freightNote: Ocean to Canadian port per 9L case. Get a current quote.

duty.basis: Canada wine customs duty ~CAD 1.87/hL for non-FTA origin; nil under CETA (EU) / CPTPP where applicable.

CA.boardMarkupNote: Provincial board ad valorem markup (e.g. LCBO Ontario ~71.5%) applied to landed cost, PLUS flat levies and container deposits, BEFORE board retail price and HST. This dominates the model in control markets — treat importer/distributor margins as N/A and model the board markup instead.
